hook: ChatGPT gave me a 7-step plan when I asked for help with one decision. I needed a yes or a no.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 7-step plan when I asked for help with one decision. I needed a yes or a no.

Here's the thing nobody talks about: AI isn't bad at helping you think. It's bad at knowing HOW you think.

Some people want frameworks. Spreadsheets. Pros and cons laid out like a legal brief.

Other people need to talk it out. Stream of consciousness. Just validate the gut feeling that's already there.

And then there's the person who just needs one blunt sentence and then silence to sit with it.

All three asked the same question. All three got the same 7-step plan.

That's not a you problem. That's a calibration problem.

When AI doesn't know your thinking style, it defaults to the most "complete" answer. Which is almost never the most useful one.

The Decisive thinker leaves every AI conversation more confused than when they started — because they got information when they needed direction.

BootFile fixes the mismatch. Your profile tells the AI: this person doesn't want options, they want a call.

Same question. Completely different response.

Which one are you — do you want the framework or the verdict?
